name: recovery-workflow
description: Machine-readable recovery workflow definition — detect corruption, attempt repair, fallback to backup, and report status.

databasePath.description: Path to the database file to recover

errorType.description: Error type: corruption, lock, timeout, disk-full, unknown

steps.name: Detect Corruption
steps.description: Run PRAGMA integrity_check to identify corruption type and severity

steps.name: Backup Current State
steps.description: Create a backup of the current (potentially corrupted) state for forensic analysis

steps.name: Attempt Repair
steps.description: Attempt to repair the database — WAL checkpoint, index rebuild, vacuum

steps.name: Verify Repair
steps.description: Run integrity_check again to confirm repair was successful

steps.name: Fallback to Backup
steps.description: If repair failed, restore from the most recent valid backup

steps.name: Fallback to Seed Data
steps.description: If no valid backup exists, recreate database from schema and seed data

steps.name: Verify Recovery
steps.description: Run integrity_check and verify table structure after recovery

steps.name: Return Recovery Status
steps.description: Return structured status — recovery method, tables restored, data integrity
